title: Cleidion ramosii
intro: Cleidion ramosii is a plant species belonging to the family Burseraceae, commonly known as the torchwood tree or Burseraceae family. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cleidion ramosii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cleidion ramosii is used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and in some cultures, it is also used as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Cleidion ramosii is classified as a species of plant in the family Burseraceae, which includes other plants like frankincense and myrrh trees. This plant is native to the Philippines and is considered an endangered species. It is a type of tree that belongs to the genus Cleidion, which is a part of the larger Burseraceae family.
